categories.name: Mathematics (Calculus)
categories.description: Calculus I and Calculus II are required.

categories.name: Mathematics (Linear Algebra)
categories.description: Choose one: AM 10 (Mathematical Methods for Engineers I) or MATH 21.

categories.name: Mathematics (Multivariate Calculus)
categories.description: Choose one: AM 30 (Multivariate Calculus for Engineers) or MATH 23A.

categories.name: Statistics
categories.description: STAT 131 (Introduction to Probability Theory) and STAT 132 (Classical and Bayesian Inference) are both required.

categories.name: General Chemistry
categories.description: Select one of the following General Chemistry series.

categories.name: Organic Chemistry
categories.description: Complete both Organic Chemistry courses (CHEM 8A and CHEM 8B).

categories.name: Biology (Required)
categories.description: Cell and Molecular Biology (BIOL 20A) is required.

categories.name: Genetics
categories.description: Choose one: BME 105 (Genetics in the Genomics Era, strongly recommended) or BIOL 105 (Genetics).

categories.name: Molecular Biology
categories.description: BME 101 (Molecular Biology for Biomolecular Engineers) is required. Select one of the following for molecular biology coverage.

categories.name: Biochemistry
categories.description: Choose one biochemistry course: CHEM 103 (Biochemistry, prereq CHEM 8B), BIOC 100A (Biochemistry and Molecular Biology, prereq BIOL 20A), or BIOL 100 (Biochemistry, prereq BIOE 20B and CHEM 8B or CHEM 112A).

categories.name: Programming (Core)
categories.description: Choose one primary programming course: BME 160 (Research Programming in the Life Sciences, 6 units) or CSE 30 (Programming Abstractions: Python, 7 units).

categories.name: Programming (Data Structures)
categories.description: Choose one data structures path: CSE 13S (Computer Systems and C Programming, 7 units) OR CSE 101P (Introduction to Data Structures and Algorithms in Python), AND CSE 101 (Introduction to Data Structures and Algorithms).

categories.name: Machine Learning (Advanced)
categories.description: Choose one: CSE 142 (Machine Learning, also listed as CSOER 142) or CSE 144 (Applied Machine Learning).

categories.name: Bioinformatics Core
categories.description: Complete all bioinformatics core courses: BME 80G (Bioethics in the 21st Century), BME 110 (Computational Biology Tools), BME 185 (Technical Writing for Biomolecular Engineers, also satisfies DC requirement), BME 163 (Applied Visualization and Analysis of Scientific Data).

categories.name: Elective
categories.description: Choose one elective. Course used as an Elective cannot be used to satisfy other major requirements. Any 5-credit biomolecular engineering graduate course (BME 201-279) may also be used.

categories.name: Bioinformatics Capstone

groups.label: Senior Thesis: BME 195 (15 units over 3 quarters)

categories.description: Choose one capstone sequence. The Bioinformatics Capstone includes BME 205 (Bioinformatics), BME 230A (Introduction to Computational Genomics and Systems Biology), and BME 129C (Project Design and Implementation in Biomolecular Engineering III). The Senior Thesis option is 15 units of BME 195 over 3 quarters.

categories.name: Disciplinary Communication (DC)
categories.description: BME 185 (Technical Writing for Biomolecular Engineers) satisfies the DC requirement. Also counted in Bioinformatics Core.
